title: EoEs user rating on imdb has gone up
display_title: EoEs user rating on imdb has gone up
wikitext:
[]: EoE's user rating on imdb has gone up             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Fri Nov 26, 2010 2:14 am This is probably the most useless topic you will ever see here, but I thought it was kind of interesting. When I first watched End of Evangelion and went to imdb and rated it, the rating was 7.9 but now, nearly six months later, it has gone up to 8.1 http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0169858/

[]:             Quote Post by Podferio » Fri Nov 26, 2010 5:06 am People get smart after some time. And maybe after watching it more than once and trying to understand the movie instead of raging because he can't make sense of what's happening, they decided to give it higher ratings.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Fri Nov 26, 2010 7:23 am Like many great works, it's stature grows with time.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Nov 26, 2010 7:40 am Chrad wrote: Like many great works, it's stature grows with time.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Fri Nov 26, 2010 8:40 am soul.assassin wrote: No, really, people at first don't/couldn't get it, hate EoE's seemingly overwhelming nihilism, and then forget it, but with time passing the same people who hated it in the first place suddenly find EoE at lot more different; they certainly wised up at a mature age.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Nov 26, 2010 9:23 am Well, EoE is more unusual than most other intellectual works because if you haven't seen the 26 episode show before watching the film you have absolutely no idea of what's even going on in the first place, much less why the film concludes the way it does. And even if they have watched the NGE series, some folks just get viewer fatigue by the time they hit EoE. So it's understandable that people wouldn't enjoy this film as much as the quality of this film would seem to dictate. I mean, that was the reason why I didn't even bother to try to understand EoE at first. I had just gotten finished watching a whole TV series that ended in this weird slide show of tormented characters, and now I'm supposed to grasp the Come Sweet Death sequence? Screw it, I'll figure it all out later. And I did! Two days after watching EoE everything inside of it just clicked and seemed to fall in place; and that was before I rewatched the film! So after that realization I bought the whole series and the movies on DVD just to enjoy them as thoroughly as I wanted to in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 26, 2010 10:51 am Rebuild has just given more visibility to it, leading more people into watching it

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Nov 26, 2010 11:40 am Xard wrote: Rebuild has just given more visibility to it, leading more people into watching it

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Nov 26, 2010 12:22 pm Xard wrote: Rebuild has just given more visibility to it, leading more people into watching it

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 26, 2010 12:30 pm Allemann wrote: Just like Do You Remember Love?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 26, 2010 12:59 pm Sachi wrote: Besides, I don't think an increase in .2 is really noteworthy. If the increase had been, say, 2 whole points, then yes, that would have been somewhat peculiar.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 26, 2010 1:12 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Uh? Actually I watched DYRL and then SDF Macross and I enjoyed SDF much more than DYRL. But actually I'm a fan of 80s/brontosaurs age animation and maybe I'm an exception (recently I've seen Orguss and it was quite nice, even the weird ending wasn't that bad after all).

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Nov 26, 2010 2:33 pm I hated EoE until Naw linked me here. I couldn't understand any of it, and I understood the TV Ending after thinking about it for five minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Nov 27, 2010 1:23 am Xard wrote: As a general rule anime fans tend to obey something akin law of "first show I see minus two years" as what they consider "new" anime. For people who start these days with Death Note or Code Geass Cowboy Bebop is already ancient show, while for me that's strictly modern/new.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:39 am 7.9 to 8.1? GOOD! In fact, it should get more than 8.1.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei Makes You Go Splat » Sat Dec 11, 2010 11:56 pm tomrule123 wrote: 7.9 to 8.1? GOOD! In fact, it should get more than 8.1.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 12, 2010 1:05 am Sachi wrote: I don't think an increase in .2 is really noteworthy.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Sun Dec 12, 2010 2:51 am This is IMDb we are talking about. There a .2 increase means a lot - especially when the rating is already close to 8. Remember that most of the Top 250 IMDb films have ratings between 8.0 and 8.5 so the rating is surprisingly high.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Dec 12, 2010 3:30 pm I don't recally for IMDB since their Top 250 and Bottom 100 can be so broken sometimes. But its nice to see EoE to have a high rating on the site though.

[]:             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Sun Dec 12, 2010 3:32 pm It makes me wonder what major critics like Maltin or Ebert would have thought of it. Too bad it has no reviews on Rotten Tomatoes... Ebert probably would have liked it because he generally has tons of respect for Japanese filmmakers, but only if he saw the series first.
